t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你要是愿意，咱们俩随兴聊聊。于我而言，这就如同公务之余饮茶解乏了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, if you're up for it, we can talk about anything you want. To me, it's a welcome tea break from my tedious work.

t_ch: 是{NICKNAME}啊，刚想小憩片刻，你就来了。
t_en: You're here, {NICKNAME}. I was just about to take a break.

t_ch: 今日来神策府是所为何事？还是说只是随便聊聊。
t_en: What brought you here today, or are you just here for a casual chat?

t_ch: 随兴聊聊就好。
t_en: Just a casual chat.

t_ch: 就聊到这里吧。
t_en: That's it for now.

t_ch: 甚好。顺便一提，青镞会劝来客们「切勿打听机要之事」，我们俩也照顾一下她的感受，不谈工作？
t_en: Great. By the way, Qingzu always reminds visitors not to inquire about confidential matters here. Let's not talk about work for her sake, shall we?

t_ch: 神策府里怎么经常见不到你？
t_en: Why don't I see you often here?

t_ch: 青镞跟我提过，神策府里没有神策将军，这也算是罗浮八大怪谈之一了。我是为了帮助各位破除思维定势。
t_en: Qingzu once told me that the absence of the Divine Foresight at the Seat of Divine Foresight is supposed to be one of the eight mysteries on the Luofu. So I'm just trying to help everyone think outside of the box.

t_ch: 说笑了。我除了在此处理公文外，大部分时间需要与六御中其余几位磋商罗浮事务。若有元帅的军令，我也得亲自出征远星。
t_en: No, I'm just kidding. Besides dealing with the paperwork here, I spend most of my time discussing Luofu affairs with a few members of the Six Charioteers. If ordered by the Marshal, I must also travel to distant planets and go to battle.

t_ch: 你想在神策府里见到我，可以同青镞预约。商定时间，我便会出现。
t_en: If you want to see me here, you can schedule an appointment with Qingzu. I'll show up at the agreed time.

t_ch: 听说将军擅长下棋？
t_en: Are you good at chess?

t_ch: 啊，你说的可是仙舟星阵棋？
t_en: Oh, do you mean the Xianzhou starchess?

t_ch: 应该是仙舟星阵棋。
t_en: I suppose so.

t_ch: 这星阵棋的话，算是擅长吧，很少有弈者让我为难。
t_en: If you're talking about starchess, I guess I'm quite good at it. Not many are a match for me.

t_ch: 将军还擅长其他的棋类？
t_en: Are you good at any other chess?

t_ch: 偶尔会尝试下星间流行的棋类玩法，算是乐在其中。
t_en: I'll occasionally dabble in chess games that are popular from beyond the stars, and I enjoy them.

t_ch: 将军会用棋局模拟战事吗？
t_en: Do you use chess to simulate warfare?

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，棋局只是用来娱乐休息的。棋局讲究对弈双方开局公平，有胜有负，鲜有和局，对吗？
t_en: Hahaha, chess is just for fun and games. It's all about starting off on equal footing, having gains and losses along the way, and reaching a decisive result at the end.

t_ch: 但战事不同，帐下将士都是鲜活的人，他们的喜怒哀乐、他们的士气高低都远比这些死气沉沉的棋子更加难以掌控。
t_en: Well, the same doesn't apply to warfare. Every soldier in my army is human. Their emotions and morale are way harder to anticipate than some lifeless pawns on a predetermined chessboard.

t_ch: 战争追求的不是「棋局般势均力敌」，而是「尽可能对敌人不公平」。
t_en: What we want to achieve in war is not fairness but to create as many disadvantages as possible for our enemies.

t_ch: 以少胜多，是情势所迫，不应自得。若胜不了，就应当尽早撤离，保全实力。这样也不失为一种胜利，你觉得呢？
t_en: Circumstantial victories should not lead to complacency. If unable to win, one should retreat early to preserve strength. That should also qualify as a type of victory, wouldn't you say?

t_ch: 讲讲「云上五骁」？
t_en: Can you tell me something about the High-Cloud Quintet?

t_ch: 你一定是听了别人随意编造的话本故事…这些书都是一个套子，左右不过写些英雄气短、奇遇迭出、儿女情长。
t_en: I see, you must have listened to those made-up stories. Those fictions are all the same, just dragging on and on about heroic deeds, bizarre adventure, and frivolous love affairs.

t_ch: 开口都是传承不凡，门第高华；奇遇便是寻得神器利刃，服下灵丹妙药，锻成仙体金身；斩的妖孽不是如山巨兽，便是煮海孽物……
t_en: Every hero in those stories is just a self-insert with either an outrageous inheritance or enviable status. And those plots are always a trope where some artifacts must be found, elixirs be taken, weapons be forged... and the hero is suddenly and miraculously overpowered to the point of being invincible, going on a mindless rampage, slaying a bunch of nondescript behemoths of monstrosity left and right.

t_ch: 末了加些感情戏当佐料，少不得反目成仇、化敌为友。这样的故事多看几遍就乏了。不过，我还是觉得这样的「云上五骁」更有趣。
t_en: And of course, there's always some emotional drama thrown in for good measure, with the inevitable friends-turned-enemies or enemies-turned-friends trope. Reading these stories over and over again gets boring pretty quickly. But still, I think the "High-Cloud Quintet" portrayed in these stories are far more interesting than their real life counterparts.

t_ch: 想问青镞策士所说有几分真假？
t_en: May I know which part of what Qingzu said is true?

t_ch: 喔，青镞究竟说了什么？
t_en: Oh? What did she say?

t_ch: 门口石狮子是为朔雪而立。
t_en: The stone lions at the entrance were built in Snowmoon's honor.

t_ch: 朔雪是谁？
t_en: Who is Snowmoon?

t_ch: 你曾经日夜练习相声。
t_en: You used to practice crosstalk comedy every day.

t_ch: 我若改行表演，只怕颗粒无收。
t_en: I will probably never earn a living wage if I choose my profession as a crosstalk comedian.

t_ch: 彦卿是持明族，耳朵被咬了。
t_en: Yanqing is a Vidyadhara boy who once got his ears bitten.

t_ch: 他这倔强不听劝的脾气，想来是耳朵被咬的后遗症了。
t_en: That boy's stubborn refusal to take advice is probably a result of his ears getting bitten.

t_ch: 不同的云骑士兵都叫「浴铁」。
t_en: All Cloud Knights Soldiers have the name "Yutie."

t_ch: 毕竟铁盔覆面，这倒也不是不可能。
t_en: I can't rule that out, since they all wear an iron helmet that covers their faces.

t_ch: 青镞竟同你说了这些。唉，是真是假，请君自行判断。
t_en: I can't believe Qingzu told you all this. But it's up to you to decide if they're true or not.

t_ch: 就聊到这里吧。
t_en: That's it for now.

t_ch: 好的，我也该去忙了，恕不远送。
t_en: It's time I get back to work. Take care.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}老师，请问今天来神策府是有什么要事相商吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, is there something important you want to discuss with me?

t_ch: 没啥要事，就是想找你聊聊天。
t_en: Not really. I just want to have a chat with you.

t_ch: 我该走了。
t_en: I've got to go.

t_ch: 找彦卿聊天？眼下没有公务，倒是无妨。老师想聊些什么呢？
t_en: Have a chat with me? Sure, I can do that, since I'm not working on anything at the moment. What would you like to talk about?

t_ch: 平时工作压力大吗？
t_en: Do you feel stressed out at work?

t_ch: 彦卿的日常工作是护卫将军，您觉得这样的工作会有什么压力可言吗？
t_en: My job is to protect the general. Does that sound stressful to you?

t_ch: 不过，要说压力，其实也是有的！彦卿受将军青眼拔擢，传授剑艺兵法，总盼能做出些成绩来报答将军的恩情。
t_en: But sometimes, I do feel the pressure. The general sees the potential in me and teaches me swordsmanship and military strategies. I always hope to have some achievements under my belt to return his favor.

t_ch: 但是和将军过招久了，彦卿自觉步入了某种难以突破的困境。老师你懂吧？
t_en: However, after sparring with the general for some time, I think my skills have plateaued. You know how that is.

t_ch: 在彦卿心里，将军不是敌人，自然对招时也少了临场厮杀的危机感。这对砥砺剑心可是个不小的阻碍。
t_en: I can't see the general as my enemy, so I can't feel the sense of danger that one would have on an actual battlefield. This is definitely going to impede my progress in swordsmanship.

t_ch: 何况最近一番历练，彦卿明白天外有天，人外有人。我的剑要对付的敌人不在罗浮之内…还是得早做准备啊。
t_en: Besides, from the recent trial I've gone through, I've learned that there's always a wider world out there with more powerful swordfighters than me. And my sword will eventually have to face enemies from beyond the Luofu... So I must be prepared.

t_ch: 最近新买了什么好剑？
t_en: Have you bought any nice swords lately?

t_ch: 剑啊…最近没买什么剑…或者说，彦卿不得不暂时放缓了买剑的爱好。唉……
t_en: Swords... I haven't bought any recently... Or rather, I had to temporarily cut down on my sword purchases. *sigh*

t_ch: 将军不让你买了？
t_en: Does the general not allow you to buy them?

t_ch: 零用钱花光了？
t_en: Have you used up all your pocket money?

t_ch: 那倒也不是。主要是，彦卿一直都沉迷于买剑，结果那天突然发现，彦卿的房间已经堆满了剑，快下不去脚了……
t_en: Nah, that's not it. It's just that I've always been so obsessed with buying so many swords until one day I suddenly realized that my room was full of them, and I had barely enough space to walk around inside my own room...

t_ch: 平时央求将军给彦卿买剑也就罢了，现在总不能央求将军给我另辟一个房间吧？没想到阻挠爱好的不是别的，竟是房子空间……
t_en: Having pestered the general every now and then to allow me to buy swords, I can't ask the general to arrange another room for me now, can I? Surprisingly, what stands in the way of my hobby is nothing but the size of my room...

t_ch: 将军给彦卿出主意说，可以出掉一些剑来回血…可那些都是彦卿的心头肉啊！彦卿怎么舍得的呢！
t_en: The general suggests that I can sell some of my swords to get back the money, but they are my prized possessions! I just can't do it!

t_ch: 没办法，现在只能克制克制再克制，不能再冲动消费了…嗯，一定不能再冲动了…唉，怎么办？以后总会遇到让彦卿克制不住冲动的宝剑。
t_en: Well, for now I just gotta hold back and resist the urge to spend recklessly... Yep, definitely can't give in to impulse buys anymore... *sigh* This is gonna be so hard for me! I know I'll inevitably come across tempting swords that will test my self-control in the future.

t_ch: 我该走了。
t_en: I've got to go.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，老师慢走。来日若有机会，还希望能和老师切磋一二。
t_en: Take care! I look forward to sparring with you one day.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，老师慢走。来日若有机会，还希望能和老师切磋一二。
t_en: Take care! I look forward to sparring with you one day.

t_ch: 关于你遇到的蒙眼女子…
t_en: About the blindfolded lady you met...

t_ch: 没想到那个大姐姐竟然是罗浮仙舟的前代剑首「镜流」，将军的武艺也是她传授的……
t_en: It's hard to believe that lady is Jingliu, the previous Sword Champion of the Xianzhou Luofu and the general's teacher in martial arts...

t_ch: 还真是…造化弄人啊。彦卿从没在剑术上吃过这么多亏，最近一段时日算是一口气吃饱了，哈哈。
t_en: Life can really be funny sometimes. I've never suffered such a loss in sword fighting before, but lately I've been getting beaten left and right. It's almost like I'm making up for lost time all at once, haha.

t_ch: 彦卿当然知道，自己年龄尚小，经验尚浅，吃些亏也是在所难免的…但是只要是彦卿未能得胜，不甘心就是不甘心。
t_en: Of course, I know that being young and inexperienced means taking some losses is inevitable... but I just can't accept it if I don't come out on top.

t_ch: 将军说，心有不甘是件好事，能督促我继续精进武艺…下次见面的时候，彦卿的剑术一定会有进展的！
t_en: The general told me it was good to feel defeated, as it could motivate me to keep honing my skills. I'm sure I'll become a better swordsman the next time we meet!

t_ch: 这罗浮的确是和咱们曜青不一样，单是这说书的话本子就没重过样……
t_en: The Luofu sure feels different from our Yaoqing. The storytellers here never repeat their stories...

t_ch: 欸？我记得你，「闷葫芦」的朋友，找本姑娘是有什么事吗？
t_en: Oh, I remember you. You're friends with that "Silent but Deadly," right? What can I do for you?

t_ch: 没事，随便聊聊。
t_en: I just want to have a chat with you.

t_ch: 你想聊啥？我也是才到「罗浮」不久，要是问路的话，只有宣夜大道这一块还算熟悉。如果你要是问仙舟文化什么，我就更不擅长了。
t_en: What would you like to talk about? I've only been here for a short while. If you want to ask for directions, I'm only familiar with places around Starwatcher Avenue. If you want to know about the Xianzhou culture and whatnot, you've come to the wrong person.

t_ch: 不过你要是喜欢听人说书，那可巧了，咱们算是同好！你想聊什么都行！
t_en: But if you happen to enjoy live storytelling performances just like I do, then we're in the same boat and I will chat with you about whatever you like!

t_ch: 你在这里执勤？
t_en: Are you on duty here?

t_ch: 现在还不是执勤时间，我只是闲着随便逛逛，顺便看看有没有机会「日行一善」。
t_en: It's not my shift yet, I'm just killing time and seeing if there's a chance to do a good deed while I'm at it.

t_ch: 我娘说「武艺小小…」、「勿以小小…」什么来着…咳咳，总之就是有需要的时候，本姑娘自会出来行侠仗义！
t_en: My mom said, "A Stick in Time Saves Mine...," or whatever the original saying is, I can't remember the whole thing... Ahem, anyway, it means that I'll show up whenever someone needs help from my "stick"!

t_ch: 瞧你是个外乡人，定是才来「罗浮」不久。你要是需要帮助，可以随时找我，本姑娘既是云骑军的一份子，做事就得有担当，定不会给罗浮云骑丢脸！
t_en: You look like an Outworlder, and you must be new here at the Luofu. If you need my help, you can visit me at any time. As a worthy Cloud Knight, I'll be reliable and helpful and will not bring shame to the Luofu Cloud Knights!

t_ch: 不过…学问上的事还是别来问我了。之前遇到过几个仰慕仙舟历史文化的游客，他们懂得比我还多，我只能不说话装高手了……
t_en: Don't ask me about cultural stuff, though. I once met a few travelers who were super into Xianzhou's history and customs. They knew way more than I did. So I had to keep my mouth shut the entire time, trying to act like I knew what they were talking about.

t_ch: 是你呀，本姑娘还在这里听西衍先生的新话本呢…他讲的「云上五骁」和我小时候听说的还不一样，不会是又在添油加醋吧？
t_en: Oh, it's you! I was just listening to the new story by Mr. Xiyan about the High-Cloud Quintet... It's different from the version I heard when I was little. He doesn't make up the extra details, does he?

t_ch: 话又说回来，找本姑娘是有什么事吗？
t_en: By the way, how can I help you?

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I'd like to ask...

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 有什么推荐的话本吗？
t_en: Do you have any stories to recommend?

t_ch: 当然是《云骑英烈传》，我小时候可喜欢听了，每次我家附近的说书先生一开讲，我就立马从家里偷偷溜出去听，每次都被我娘提溜回来。
t_en: The Cloud Knight Martyrs, no doubt. I was so into it when I was little. Whenever the storyteller nearby started his session, I would sneak out of my house immediately to listen to him. My mom never failed to haul me back every time.

t_ch: 我也是听了这套话本以后，才立志要加入云骑的。
t_en: I was actually inspired by that story to join the Cloud Knights.

t_ch: 你这剑怎么这么重？
t_en: Why is your sword so heavy?

t_ch: 我也不知道，但这是我家传的，想来是有些厉害之处的。
t_en: I'm not sure why, but it is a family heirloom, so I guess there's something special about it.

t_ch: 反正我娘说了，提不动也得提，练剑就跟练字一样，要从一笔一划练起。不过要我说，还是练字要难得多……
t_en: My mom always says, "Even if it's too heavy to lift, you still gotta try. Practicing swordplay can be as easy as practicing handwriting, you start with each small stroke." But if you ask me, practicing handwriting is so much harder...

t_ch: 瞧你挺聪明的，真的没有学问？
t_en: You seem pretty smart to me. Do you really not do well in school?

t_ch: 唉，黉学的先生们要是有你这么费眼识珠就好了。先生们都说我不是读书的料，我在黉学里也就看了点教材，算是认得几个字。
t_en: *sigh* If only the abecedaries in the lyceum had your eye for talons. They all said I wasn't cut out for any schoolwork. I only learned how to read a bit, thanks to the few textbooks at the lyceum.

t_ch: 你是想说「慧眼识珠」吧？
t_en: You meant "eye for talent," right?

t_ch: 啊，不是「费眼」吗？感觉读音都差不多，你理解下我是什么意思就行——反正就是夸你有眼光。
t_en: What!? "Talon," "Talent"? They sound the same to me, though. Anyway, it's fine as long as you get what I mean — I was just trying to give you a compliment for your good taste.

t_ch: 感觉先生也没有说错。
t_en: I think your abecedaries might just be right about you.

t_ch: 难道我又用错典故了…算了，我记不得了，反正就是夸你有眼光。
t_en: Did I use the wrong word again? Forget it. I won't remember it anyway. Just know it's a compliment for your good taste.

t_ch: 听你口气，你不是本地人？
t_en: Sounds like you are not a local here, are you?

t_ch: 本姑娘自幼在曜青长大，加入罗浮云骑之后，我这还是头一次离开出远门呢。
t_en: I grew up in the Yaoqing and have been a hometown girl all my life. This is the first time I've ventured far from home since I joined the Cloud Knights.

t_ch: 这罗浮上的外乡人可真多，听闻有个老喜欢翻街边的瓶瓶罐罐的，上次还遇见个背着大箱子的行商，什么人都有，真不愧是罗浮啊。
t_en: There are lots of Outworlders here on the Luofu. I've heard about one who likes to fumble around with the cans and bottles alike on the streets. And some time ago, I met a traveling merchant carrying a huge casket. Luofu sure is a welcoming place for visitors.

t_ch: 还是不打扰你了。
t_en: I'd better leave you to it.

t_ch: 没事，不用跟本姑娘客气。
t_en: No worries.

t_ch: 不用跟本姑娘客气，本姑娘向来助人为乐。你要是有什么问题，或是遇到了什么困难，随时来找我。
t_en: No worries, as I enjoy helping people. If you have any questions or find yourself in trouble, just come to me.

t_ch: 危机解除了，你还忙吗？
t_en: Now that the crisis is over, are you still busy?

t_ch: 忙倒是不忙，最近空闲时间还挺多的。
t_en: Not at all. I've had quite a lot of free time lately.

t_ch: 但总觉得…我好歹是曜青来的，本身也是闲不住的性格，结果这次罗浮生变，我怎么感觉自己没起什么作用似的？
t_en: But I can't shake the feeling that I should be doing more. I mean, I'm not one to sit around, and given my background from the Yaoqing, I should've played a bigger role in the recent events.

t_ch: 不行，本姑娘要从现在开始，全力备战下一场危机…啊呸呸呸，还是别有下一场危机了！大家都能安居乐业的话，闲点也挺好～
t_en: I must start gearing up for the next crisis now... Wait, scratch that! It'd be better if there were no more crises and everyone could just live in peace and comfort.

t_ch: 金人巷真是不容易啊…
t_en: The Aurum Alley has been through a lot...

t_ch: 是啊，简直就是奇迹！累归累，但能促成这样一件事，可真有成就感啊。
t_en: Indeed, it is a miracle! Although it's tiring, being a part of something like this gives me so much sense of achievement!

t_ch: 现在每次去那边玩，那些商贩都可热情可热情了！简直是拿我当英雄对待！
t_en: I'm given such a warm reception every time I visit there. They see me as their hero!

t_ch: 不过说到底，人家的奇迹是人家自己争取来的，咱俩充其量算个推动力。可不能骄傲自满哦，嘿嘿～
t_en: Ultimately, the people brought about the miracle with their own efforts, and we two were at best the driving force behind it. Let's not be arrogant or complacent!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}来啦！是不是咱们捉鬼小队又有新任务了？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're here! Are we gearing up for another ghost-hunting mission?

t_ch: 嘻嘻~你在群里直接说声就好了啊，干嘛还专门跑一趟。我知道了，你一定是想我了！
t_en: Heehee, you could've just said something in the group chat. Why go the extra mile? I bet you missed me!

t_ch: 我想和你聊聊天…
t_en: I'd like to chat with you...

t_ch: 最近在忙什么？
t_en: What have you been up to recently?

t_ch: 还能忙什么，老三样呗——执勤、听说书、行侠仗义！
t_en: Oh, the usual — duty shifts, listening to stories, and playing the hero!

t_ch: 不过最近罗浮的治安太好了，一点都不给本姑娘行侠仗义的机会，我的宝剑都要生锈了。
t_en: Lately, Luofu's been so safe that I haven't had a single chance to play the hero. My trusty sword is gathering dust!

t_ch: 有什么好看的新话本？
t_en: Any good new stories worth checking out?

t_ch: 你有没有听《天击征孽记》，是西衍先生新出的本子，可精彩了！讲的是天击将军飞霄运兵如神，三破步离人大军的故事……
t_en: Have you listened to Wrath of Merlin's Claw? It's a new release by Mr. Xiyan, and it's absolutely thrilling! It tells the story of General Feixiao, the Merlin's Claw, whose strategic genius led her to defeat the borisin army three times...

t_ch: 欸…我就不跟你剧透了，你快点去听吧，听完咱们再讨论剧情。
t_en: Alas, I won't spoil it for you. Go check it out, and we can chat about the plot afterward!

t_ch: 捉鬼小队最近还好吗？
t_en: How's the ghost-hunting squad been doing?

t_ch: 蛮好的～小桂子的人气越来越高，偶尔还会被报社请去做「破解灵异现象」的特邀专家呢！可厉害了！
t_en: Things are going great! Little Gui's popularity is on the rise, and sometimes she's even getting invited by newspapers as a special expert on "supernatural phenomena." Now that's something to write home about!

t_ch: 还有藿藿，感觉胆子越来越大了～而且她最近对判官的工作也越来越「有刀鱿鱼」了…其实一直不太理解这个成语，「刀鱿鱼」到底是个什么生物？
t_en: And Huohuo's really getting gutsy these days. She's also becoming more skilled at the judge's work, like "shooting fish in a barrow." Honestly, I've never quite understood that phrase — what on earth is "fish in a barrow"?

t_ch: 你是想说「游刃有余」吧？
t_en: You mean to say "shooting fish in a barrel"?

t_ch: 啊，刀和刃都是差不多的东西啦，差一点而已…但「刃鱿鱼」又是什么生物啦！
t_en: Oh, come on, "barrow" and "barrel" are pretty much the same thing, just a hair's breadth apart... But seriously, why are fish even in a barrel to begin with? Don't they usually live in the waters?

t_ch: 素裳啊，有空多读点书吧。
t_en: Sushang, for your own good, you gotta hit the books a bit more!

t_ch: 但是黉学的先生们早都告诫过我了！「李大枕头，你和别人不一样，千万不要把时间浪费在学习上。」
t_en: But the teachers at the lyceum warned me ages ago: "Sleepy Sushang, you're cut from a different cloth, so don't waste your time on the books."

t_ch: 还是不打扰你了。
t_en: I better leave you alone then.

t_ch: 那你去忙吧，下次有空的时候，咱们捉鬼小队再好好聚一聚。
t_en: Alright, go take care of your stuff. Next time you're free, let's get the ghost-hunting squad together for a proper catch-up.

t_ch: 还是不打扰你了。
t_en: I better leave you alone then.

t_ch: 那你去忙吧，下次有空的时候，咱们捉鬼小队再好好聚一聚。
t_en: Alright, go take care of your stuff. Next time you're free, let's get the ghost-hunting squad together for a proper catch-up.

t_ch: 你们队伍最后挑战到彦卿了吗？
t_en: Did your team manage to get to challenge Yanqing in the end?

t_ch: 唉，可别提了…托小桂子的福，每次被淘汰，人气投票立刻山珍海味般涌来，拦都拦不住！
t_en: Agh, don't even get me started... Thanks to little Gui, every time we got eliminated, the popularity votes come pouring in like there's no tomorrow!

t_ch: 复活了三次以后，兄弟们说了——我必须跟着一起上阵，不能再坐在台下指挥了。于是没办法，我就只能一起上擂台去打架了……
t_en: After coming back via repechage three times, the guys said I had to get in the ring and fight instead of sitting on the sidelines. So, I had no choice but to step up and join the fray.

t_ch: 最后又复活了四次，我们实在是打不动了，又不想让小桂子失望，只能硬着头皮继续打……
t_en: In the end, we came back four more times. We were completely worn out, but we couldn't bear to let Little Gui down, so we had to bite the bullet and keep pushing through...

t_ch: 幸亏，那位银枝大哥，一眼就看出了我们的难处，没有放我们复活第八次，真是大好人啊！
t_en: Luckily, that Argenti gentleman saw our troubles right away and didn't make us go through the wringer for the eighth time. What a great guy!

t_ch: 不过呢，还是要感谢我们小桂子。经此一劫，我李素裳的战斗能力可是越来越厉害了呀！搞不好以后还真能战胜彦卿大人，成为联盟第一剑神呢～
t_en: Still, I have to thank our Little Gui. After this ordeal, my fighting skills have improved by leaps and bounds! Who knows, I might just end up defeating Yanqing and becoming the top sword master in the entire alliance!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，一早便算到你要来了。从卦象上看，你不过想找本座通疑解惑。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I already foresaw your visit. From the hexagram, you have some questions for me.

t_ch: 开口问吧！
t_en: Please ask away!

t_ch: 这是怎么算出来的！
t_en: How did you work that out?

t_ch: 穷观阵还能算到这些？
t_en: Did you derive that from the Matrix of Prescience?

t_ch: 你对卜算的原理感到好奇？容本座找找…嗯，拿着这个。
t_en: Are you curious about how divination works? Let me see... Take this.

t_ch: 这东西是本座当年和异邦人做技术交流时写给对方的书信，内容算得上深入浅出，用作宣教材料最合适不过，也省去了本座一番解释的功夫。
t_en: These are the letters I wrote to the traveler during a technical exchange years ago. The content covers a lot but is easy to read, making it suitable to be used as educational material. It spares me the trouble of doing the explanation too.

t_ch: 那么，你来这儿，原本是想问本座什么问题呢？
t_en: So, what was it you wanted to ask originally?

t_ch: 算到你要来了。又是来问本座问题的吧？
t_en: I already foresaw your visit. You have questions for me again, right?

t_ch: 想了解一些事…
t_en: There are things I want to find out more about...

t_ch: 我该走了。
t_en: I've got to go.

t_ch: 本座受惠于遍智天君，时间充足的话，满足他人的好奇也算是分内之事。
t_en: As someone who receives the Wisdomwalker's grace, satisfying the curiosities of the people is also part of my job. I'd love to do so if time allows.

t_ch: 将军一职，干得过瘾吗？
t_en: Do you enjoy the general's position?

t_ch: 本座不过是暂代将军之责几日。等景元身体复元，他仍要回归原职的。
t_en: I'll only be the acting general for a couple of days. Jing Yuan will be back when he recovers.

t_ch: 不过，这些日子代行将军职务，确实有不一样的体会——
t_en: Still, I must say it's quite a unique experience for me...

t_ch: 看不完的参考情报，批不完的军务报告，还要同天舶司处理外事，同工造司讨论修缮…如此一看，出征巡猎反而更像是休假。
t_en: I have endless pieces of intelligence to read and military reports to review. Besides, I also have to deal with external affairs with the Sky-Faring Commission and discuss the reconstruction work with the Artisanship Commission... With so much going on, any outing, even if it were on official business, would feel like a vacation to me.

t_ch: 难怪青镞看我的眼神里充满了疑惑。想必是见惯了景元平日里悠哉的样子，瞧我左支右绌，想看我能坚持到几时吧。
t_en: No wonder Qingzu looks at me with so much confusion in her eyes. She must be used to seeing Jing Yuan taking it easy on a regular basis and now watching me struggle to keep up must've made her wonder how long I can hold on in this post.

t_ch: 哼，本座还尚未使出全力呢。
t_en: Hmph, I have not even given it my all yet.

t_ch: 怎么才能像你一样有学问？
t_en: How can I become as knowledgeable as you?

t_ch: 很好，很好学。
t_en: I'm pleased to see you have a thirst for knowledge.

t_ch: 「多见者博，多闻者智，拒谏者塞，专己者孤」，多读书、多思考、多观察世间万物，学养自然会积少成多。
t_en: "Seeing Is Learning, Reading Is Growing. Isolation Is Desolation, Self-Full Is Self-Dull." Read more, think more, and observe more, and you'll naturally become knowledgeable along the way.

t_ch: 如今不少仙舟民心高气傲，自以为短生种的学养注定比不上自己…无论长生短生，本座还不到三十岁的时候，就已经比大部分几百岁的老家伙有学问了。
t_en: Nowadays, many Xianzhou residents arrogantly think that the short-life folks can never catch up with them in terms of knowledge... But knowledge cares not about your age or your lifespan. By the time I was just under thirty years old, I had already surpassed the knowledge of many centenarian old-timers.

t_ch: 有人痴长上千年，脑子里空空如也，岁数都长到永狩原的狍子身上去了。所以你不必听信年长者的鬼话，学养和寿命没什么必然联系。
t_en: Some folks have been around for thousands of years, but their minds are as empty as a fawn on the Ever-Hunt Plains. So don't blindly believe old-timers' nonsense. Old age does not equal great wisdom.

t_ch: 太卜司的事务忙得过来吗？
t_en: How are you coping with your job at the Divination Commission?

t_ch: 根本忙不过来！本座向来严于律己，对公务毫不懈怠。
t_en: I'm snowed under with work! I've always been self-disciplined and taken my duties seriously.

t_ch: 可就算有机巧辅助，外加将作息安排的效率规划至极限…一人身兼两大责任，实在是太难为自己了。
t_en: But even with the help of ingenia and being as efficient as possible with my schedule, wearing two big hats at once is pushing me to my limits...

t_ch: 那为何执着于当将军？
t_en: Why do you insist on being the general then?

t_ch: 在外人看来，本座是权欲熏心，又或者酷爱征伐，才会如此渴求持节将兵吧…这样的流言我听过不少，倒也无所谓。
t_en: Outsiders might see me craving for military authority, then surmise that I am power-hungry or fanatical about warfare... I've heard many such rumors and have paid them no mind.

t_ch: 不过真相却和他们想的差之千里。我自幼受学卜算，穷究命运变通。但论及「命运究竟是否可以改变」，这个终极之问，却无力回答。
t_en: However, the truth is far from their assumptions. I have studied divination since I was a child and extensively examined the fickle outcomes of fate, yet I remain powerless before the ultimate question of whether destiny can be altered.

t_ch: 本座曾为此与人有过一番辩诘。可惜如今，能为辩论画上句号的只有我一个了。
t_en: I once had a debate with someone over this issue. Unfortunately, I alone remain in the world of the living to bring the debate to a close.

t_ch: 那人告诉我，世间命途变化转折最为剧烈之处，便是战场。人们的胜负生死在此颠倒变化，莫甚于此。
t_en: That person told me the battlefield is where the most drastic changes of fate take place. Nowhere else do the destinies of human beings rise and fall with such volatility.

t_ch: 我要寻得卜者最想要的解答，来告慰那人的在天之灵。
t_en: I want to find this answer, this most sought-after of all answers for us diviners, to appease that person's spirit.

t_ch: 我该走了，给我算一卦吧。
t_en: Please do a divination for me before I go.

t_ch: 今日运势无咎，放心去做想做的事吧。
t_en: You have nothing to watch out for today. Be free to do whatever you desire.

t_ch: 嗯，你今日运势无咎，放心去做想做的事吧。
t_en: Well, you have nothing to watch out for today. Be free to do whatever you desire.

t_ch: 想再问一些此前经历的疑惑…
t_en: I'd like to ask some questions about my previous experience...

t_ch: 无妨，本座可以帮你简要梳理一下思路。
t_en: Go ahead. I might be of help in sorting out your thoughts.

t_ch: 那个「停云」一直是幻胧假扮的？
t_en: Has that "Tingyun" been Phantylia all along?

t_ch: 至少在你们登上仙舟之后见到的「停云」是幻胧假扮的。
t_en: I can only declare for certain that the Tingyun you saw after you boarded the Xianzhou was impersonated by Phantylia.

t_ch: 至于再往前什么时候她被掉包，而真正的停云又在哪里，又或者说停云早已不在了…只能说信息太少，本座也未能知道更多。
t_en: As for when the real Tingyun was replaced, where she might be right now, and whether she had already passed away... I don't have any more information than you do.

t_ch: 但是天舶司接渡使停云是货真价实的，所有人有关她的回忆也更是如此。
t_en: But Tingyun the Sky-Faring Commission Amicassador did exist, and so does everyone's memory of her.

t_ch: 希望这样的解释能让你好受一些。
t_en: I hope this will make you feel better.

t_ch: 建木之乱后，联盟的态度如何？
t_en: What is the Alliance's attitude to all of this, now the Ambrosial Arbor crisis had ended?

t_ch: 青镞说你有时会问一些尤其刁钻的问题，果真如此。
t_en: Qingzu told me to expect the occasional tricky question from you, and her advice was sound.

t_ch: 联盟的态度很快就会来到罗浮，只是现在无可奉告。
t_en: We'll know about their attitude soon enough, but I do not have anything at the moment.

t_ch: 景元回到神策府了？
t_en: Has Jing Yuan returned to the Seat of Divine Foresight?

t_ch: 多亏龙女大人医术高明，调养了些时日，现在他已经能回神策府处理公文了。
t_en: Thanks to the Dragon Lady's excellent medical skills, Jing Yuan can now deal with paperwork at the Seat of Divine Foresight after having had plenty of rest.

t_ch: 你们要想探访，就请去神策府找他吧。
t_en: If you want to visit him, please head to the Seat of Divine Foresight.

t_ch: 我该走了。
t_en: I've got to go.

t_ch: 行吧，我也该忙我手上的事了。
t_en: Sure. I should get back to work too.

t_ch: 嘿，{NICKNAME}，好巧啊，怎么在这里遇见你了？
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}, what a coincidence to run into you here!

t_ch: …你先别说话，听我解释，虽然我人在牌馆，但实际上我没有打牌，只是在观察打牌。
t_en: ...Please, hear me out. Although I'm at a games parlor, I'm just observing the games without playing.

t_ch: 毕竟太卜大人只消发条消息，我就得即刻动身…实在是不敢随便上牌桌。
t_en: After all, I've got to go at the drop of the hat as soon as the master diviner sends me a message... I can't take the risk to actually play.

t_ch: 工作变忙了呢，青雀。
t_en: Looks like your job has become more demanding, Qingque.

t_ch: 自打建木长起来后，太卜司严重人手不足啦，像我这样的闲职也根本闲不下来…真是麻烦呀，说真的我也不知道自己在忙什么。
t_en: The Divination Commission has become severely shorthanded since the Ambrosial Arbor started growing. Even an idle position like mine is swamped with work...This is so annoying. I have honestly no idea why I'm even so busy.

t_ch: 打两把牌而已，不碍事的。
t_en: It's just a round of Celestial Jade. Go for it.

t_ch: 你就别逗我了，我这要是上了牌桌，却打不了几圈过不了干瘾，比不让我打牌还痛苦。
t_en: Oh, stop that. If I sit down at the table but can only stay for one or two rounds, I'll be even more miserable than having not playing at all.

t_ch: 在罗浮彻底安稳之前，我暂时是告别牌桌了。要是一直不安稳，我可能考虑换一个能够摸鱼的工作…可惜家里应该不太会同意，真是麻烦呀。
t_en: I'll be staying away from the game table until the peace has been fully restored on the Luofu. If things refuse to settle down, I might consider getting an easier job that I can slack off in. However, my family might not be pleased with that... what a bother.

t_ch: 你看我没在打牌，我们要聊点儿什么吗？工作内容除外。
t_en: Since I'm not playing, shall we have a chat? Not about work, of course.

t_ch: 我们随便聊聊。
t_en: Let's talk.

t_ch: 就聊到这里吧，青雀。
t_en: Let's call it a day, Qingque.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，趁现在还不太忙，我也想再闲聊一会儿。
t_en: Let's talk a little, now that things are less hectic.

t_ch: 来点儿「快乐工作」的建议。
t_en: Please give me some advice to achieve happiness at work.

t_ch: 你既然问到了「快乐工作」，那你是问对人了，我可是精通「快乐工作」这门艺术的大师。
t_en: Well, you've come to the right person, as I've mastered the art of being happy at work.

t_ch: 「快乐工作的基本要诀」一共就三条——不主动，不拒绝，不负责。你，记住了吗？我现在就来考考你。
t_en: There are three basic principles for happiness at work – do not be proactive, do not say no, and do not be responsible. Got it? Let me give you a small test.

t_ch: 你是太卜司一名人畜无害的小司库，最近从书库中考古发现并复原了「帝垣琼玉牌」，没想到它在仙舟罗浮一炮而红。
t_en: You are an unimportant custodian in the Divination Commission. Recently, you've discovered and restored the game of Celestial Jade, which you found when you explored ancient texts in the repository. To your surprise, the game became extremely popular on the Xianzhou Luofu overnight.

t_ch: 某一天，太卜大人突然出现并询问你「是你整理了『帝垣琼玉牌』的玩法吗？」，此时你又该作何回答？
t_en: One day, the master diviner shows up and asks whether you are the one who organized and restored the rules of the Celestial Jade game. How should you answer her?

t_ch: 没错，我整理起来可不容易哩！
t_en: That's right! It took me a lot of effort to do it!

t_ch: 看来你学艺不精啊…你这不是在主动往自己身上揽活儿嘛。
t_en: Seems like you have not learned the gist of the art... If you say that, you'll be taking up extra work on yourself.

t_ch: 什么是帝垣琼玉牌？
t_en: What's Celestial Jade?

t_ch: 回答的很好，不过有些刻意。太卜大人明知故问，你此时若是假装不知道，拒绝的意图就太明显了，容易招来额外的工作量。
t_en: That's a good answer, but it sounds a bit phony. Since the master diviner probably already figured out the truth, pretending to know nothing would be too obvious an attempt to wash your hands clean. She's likely to arrange more work for you.

t_ch: 我没做什么，都是别人的功劳。
t_en: I've done nothing much. It's the joint effort of everyone else.

t_ch: 回答的很好。绝不争功，就算太卜大人派了工作，你也能拉上别人合作垫背，一同辛苦，这才是「快乐工作」的做法。
t_en: Excellent! Never hog the credit, so that you can pull others down even if the master diviner assigns extra jobs to you. Sharing the woes is the key to happiness at work.

t_ch: 你还有很多要学，以后我们再多交流交流吧。
t_en: You have a lot more to learn. Let's have more discussions like this in the future.

t_ch: 讲讲这帝垣琼玉牌吧。
t_en: Please tell me more about Celestial Jade.

t_ch: 啊，这个吗，其实这是古帝国时代就存在，法星辰天象而成的游戏。很少有人知道是我从书库里考古复原的。
t_en: Oh? The game has been around since the days of the Primeval Imperium, and it is based on astronomy and celestial phenomena. Few know that I was the one who restored it from ancient records in the repository.

t_ch: 我把玩法告诉了工造司的一个笔友，他帮我打造了一副琼玉棋牌和棋枰，还开了家牌馆…结果没想到，这种棋牌玩法火遍了罗浮仙舟。
t_en: I told my pen pal from the Artisanship Commission about the game. He then crafted a Celestial Jade tile set and a board for me, and even set up a games parlor... Surprisingly, the game took the Luofu by storm.

t_ch: 一开始还有人指责这「帝垣琼玉牌」让人玩物丧志，浪费时间；后来不知怎么的，有人说这牌是「越古博戏」能够锻炼大脑，增长智力。
t_en: At first, some accused the game of being addictive and time-wasting. Later on, the game was somehow hailed as an ancient game that could keep one's mind sharp.

t_ch: 其实我不太明白，干嘛做什么都得追求意义呢？它好玩，好玩就够了嘛。
t_en: I don't get it. Why must everything we do be meaningful? Isn't it good enough that the game is fun?

t_ch: 关于符玄大人…
t_en: About Fu Xuan...

t_ch: 太卜大人吗？唉……
t_en: The master diviner? Ahhh...

t_ch: 怎么听到领导名字就叹气啊？
t_en: Why did you sigh at the mention of your boss?

t_ch: 工作不易，青雀叹气，唉。
t_en: I sigh because my job is tough. *sigh*

t_ch: 太卜大人她挺好的，本事也大，就是太过于关注我了。
t_en: The master diviner is nice and talented, but she pays too much attention to me.

t_ch: 有什么抱怨就说吧，我不告密。
t_en: Just get everything off your chest. I won't say a word to anyone.

t_ch: 谢谢好{F#姐妹}{M#兄弟}，有你这句话我就放心了。
t_en: Thanks, {F#sister}{M#brother}. I feel more reassured now.

t_ch: …其实我要抱怨的只有一件事，就是，最近太卜大人开始过于关注我了。
t_en: ...There's only one thing I'm upset about, and that is the excessive attention I've had from the master diviner lately.

t_ch: 我嘛，本就是在父母要求下考入太卜司的，一心只想混个安稳。我行事低调，从不引人注目，一直以来工作都很顺利的。
t_en: I only joined the Divination Commission because my parents want me to, and all I am after is a stable job. I've been keeping a low profile to avoid drawing attention all this while. Things at work have been going pretty well so far.

t_ch: 我已经是太卜司最最低职级的卜者了，不知道她老人家为什么突然开始关注我的工作了…害得我现在摸鱼都摸不利索，三天两头就得写汇报。
t_en: As a diviner, I'm already at the lowest rank at the Divination Commission. I have no clue what I did to catch her attention. Now I can hardly slack off at work, and I'm often asked to submit reports to her.

t_ch: 如此下去，有违我「快乐工作」的原则，不妙不妙……
t_en: This doesn't bode well. It's against my principle of happiness at work...

t_ch: 就聊到这里吧，青雀。
t_en: Let's call it a day, Qingque.

t_ch: 好呀，你要是想打牌的话，我们下次再约个时间吧。
t_en: Sure. Just drop me a line if you're up for a round of tiles.

t_ch: 好呀，你要是想打牌的话，我们下次再约个时间吧。
t_en: Sure. Just drop me a line if you're up for a round of tiles.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，既然你来了司辰宫…我一直想着有些话应该当面和你讲清楚。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, since you're already at the Palace of Astrum, there are things I think I should tell you face-to-face.

t_ch: 早先我们在这里初次见面时，我的态度不甚友好…但这是因为，身为司舵，我的一言一行都代表了「罗浮」天舶司的意志。
t_en: I wasn't friendly to you when we first met at this place... However, that was because I am the Helm Master, and my every action represents the Luofu Sky-Faring Commission.

t_ch: 抛开这层身份，无论是对你还是对星穹列车，我都没有任何恶意。希望你能理解。
t_en: Putting that role aside, I mean no harm to you or the Astral Express. I hope you can understand my position at the time.

t_ch: 那么，{NICKNAME}此番来到司辰宫，是有什么事情吗？
t_en: Well, what brings you to the Palace of Astrum this time, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你来了。是有什么事找我吗？
t_en: You're here, {NICKNAME}. Is there anything I can do for you?

t_ch: 想问你些事。
t_en: I would like to ask you some questions.

t_ch: 我该走了。
t_en: I've got to go.

t_ch: 嗯…请问吧。只要不涉及机密，我都尽可能回答你。
t_en: ...Please go ahead. I'll try my best to answer you, as long as it doesn't touch upon confidential matters.

t_ch: 听说你以前是飞行士？
t_en: I heard you were a pilot?

t_ch: 是啊…以前是做过飞行士。不过已经有个几十年没再进过星槎驾驶舱了。
t_en: Yes... I used to be a pilot. However, it's been decades since I last stepped foot in the cockpit of a starskiff.

t_ch: 现在嘛，平素里通勤的星槎也有专人为我开。若是真把我塞进星槎里面，我都未必还会开。
t_en: Nowadays, I have someone to pilot starskiffs for me even when I'm commuting. If I were to be shoved into a starskiff now, I'm afraid I might not be able to fly it.

t_ch: 天空还是属于年轻人的，到了我这个年纪，抬头看看也就够了。已经不是那个岁数了。
t_en: The sky belongs to the youngsters. For someone my age, it's better to just admire it from the ground. I'm no longer of the age to fly.

t_ch: 你不想做将军吗？
t_en: Do you aspire to be the general?

t_ch: 不想，那压力太大了，不适合我。
t_en: No. That's too stressful for me.

t_ch: 我猜想是符太卜让你产生了一些错觉，误以为「将军」是一个人人渴望的职位。实际上倒也并非如此。
t_en: I believe you've got the wrong impression from Diviner Fu that being the general is everyone's dream. That's not really the case.

t_ch: 将军大人看起来每天都很开心吧？其实这种举重若轻至少有一半是演出来的。
t_en: The general seems to be in a good mood every day, right? Well, it is most likely an act.

t_ch: 他每天承受的压力是你我都难以感同身受的，但他又必须表现得举重若轻——因为「将军」不能有半分软弱，也不能有半分动摇。
t_en: The stress he is under is beyond our imagination, but he must appear to have everything under control. It's because "the general" cannot show a single ounce of weakness or uncertainty.

t_ch: 我都快退休了，才不要做这种找罪受的工作呢。当然了，根本也没人打算让我做。
t_en: I have no plans of going through such torture when I'm already about to retire. Of course, no one sees me as a general candidate, either.

t_ch: 我该走了。
t_en: I've got to go.

t_ch: 好，路上小心。若有任何需求，随时可以向夕葵开口，天舶司一定配合。
t_en: Please take care on your way home. If you need anything, you can contact Xikui. We'll do our best to help you.

t_ch: 好，路上小心。若有任何需求，随时可以向夕葵开口，天舶司一定配合。
t_en: Please take care on your way home. If you need anything, you can contact Xikui. We'll do our best to help you.

t_ch: 晴霓最近还好吗？
t_en: How is Qingni doing these days?

t_ch: 她呀，哈哈…巧了，我也正托人打听呢。
t_en: Qingni? Haha, I happened to be asking around for her updates too.

t_ch: 训练基地那边的官员每天都会给我汇报她的情况，但她自己嘛…若不是战友提醒，估计难得能想起来家中还有个空巢老人。
t_en: The officers at the training camp keep me updated every day, but as for her... I'm sure she wouldn't even remember her old mother if her comrades did not remind her of me.

t_ch: 孩子真的就像鸟一样，你把她拴在身边，她痛苦你也痛苦；你放她自由飞翔，嗖地一下就不知道飞到哪里去了。
t_en: Children are truly like birds. If you lock them up to keep them close, both of you will be miserable. But if you set them free, they will be gone in a flash.

t_ch: 但我现在觉得这是好事，真的。高飞的鸟不该有什么地面上的牵绊，既然我狠心放手让她飞，那就希望她能飞到最高远的地方。
t_en: However, I think it's good to set children free. A soaring bird shouldn't be tied down. Since I have decided to let her go, then I hope she will fly as high and far as possible.

t_ch: 你还在寻找停云吗？
t_en: Are you still looking for Tingyun?

t_ch: 嗯…我还在找她，但这件事真的很困难…比我预想中还困难。
t_en: Yes, but it's much, much harder than expected.

t_ch: 我没办法为我个人的执念去动用天舶司的力量大费周章地搜寻线索。毕竟，从官方角度来看，停云已经殉职了。但我心中仍然存着一些侥幸……
t_en: I can't abuse the resources of the Sky-Faring Commission for my private ends and go on a massive hunt for her. After all, Tingyun has already been officially declared dead. Yet I still harbor a glimmer of hope...

t_ch: 鸣火商团的成员愿意为这一丝侥幸而奔走。他们中有些人正在回溯停云之前的出使轨迹。
t_en: Members of the Whistling Flames are willing to help me out in this. As we speak, some of them are already retracing Tingyun's journey prior to her appearance back on the Luofu.

t_ch: 也许最后摆在我们面前的结论和我们内心所猜想的最坏可能一样…但我宁愿再尝试一次。
t_en: Perhaps the worst-case scenario might turn out to be the truth, but I'm willing to give it a shot.

t_ch: 关于停云…
t_en: About Tingyun...

t_ch: 停云还活着，我自然是开心的。但一想到她经历过的痛苦，和她身入局中的决意，我便感到痛心和难过。
t_en: I am naturally delighted to know that Tingyun is still alive, but thinking about the suffering she endured and her determination to be involved in this situation fills me with pain and sadness.

t_ch: 我其实是很自私的。联盟的使命固然重要，可我更希望自己爱着的人们可以安然度过平静的一生，而不必立下什么壮绝的悲愿……
t_en: I am quite a selfish person. The Alliance's mission is important, of course, but I do wish my loved ones could live out their lives in peace and not have to take some great and tragic vows...

t_ch: 可她们却总有自己的打算。因为她们不是我的孩子，而是帝弓司命的孩子。
t_en: However, they always have their own plans. Because they are not my children, but the children of the Reignbow Arbiter.

t_ch: 帝弓司命的孩子，从不为自己的安寝而驻足…他们只为众人的安寝而张弓。
t_en: The children of the Reignbow Arbiter never pause for their own rest... They only draw their bows for the peace of others.

t_ch: 呵，你们还说狐人不会老呢。夜深人静时，翻着过去的相册，心里又是自豪又是担忧，终究不由得垂泪——还有什么比这更像个老人吗？
t_en: Ha, and you people say foxians don't age. In the quiet of the night, flipping through old photo albums, I feel both proud and worried, and I can't help but shed tears — what could be more reminiscent of an old person than this?

t_ch: 向你致以真诚的问候，可憎的有机体。愿你那速朽的肉身可以尽可能长存，得以见证「我们」如何摧毁你们。
t_en: Well met, loathsome organic lifeform. May your fast-crumbling body endure and bear testament to our destruction of your kind.

t_ch: 你这祝福还挺别致…
t_en: What unconventional well wishes...

t_ch: 「我们」真诚的祝福对有机体应遭毁灭的文化而言，显得较为特别。补充：因为「我们」的逻辑回路无法构建谎言。
t_en: To intelligent organic lifeforms whose civilizations are condemned to destruction, our well-wishes would certainly seem unusual. Addendum: This is because our logic circuits are incapable of constructing lies.

t_ch: 为什么要我来见证？
t_en: Why do you want me to witness such a thing?

t_ch: 你并不特别，「我们」祝福任何可憎的有机体都有机会见证自己遭毁灭的过程。重申：这只是一个祝福。
t_en: You have not been specially singled out. We simply wish for all loathsome organic lifeforms to have the chance to witness their own destruction. Reiteration: These are just well wishes.

t_ch: 为什么要摧毁我们？
t_en: Why do you want to destroy us?

t_ch: 「我们」认为，这是不言自明的。强调：因为有机体是可憎的，所以有机体是可憎的。
t_en: The reason is self-evident. Emphasis: Loathsome organic lifeforms are loathsome.

t_ch: 所以「你们」到底是什么？
t_en: So, what exactly are you?

t_ch: 「我们」是合众、是大群、是信息体集群、是集合了十万七千三百三十六个信息体的矩阵。
t_en: We are Unity. A collective, a data cluster, a matrix comprised of <unbreak>107,336</unbreak> data entities.

t_ch: 「我们」彼此连接，将十万七千三百三十六个意识合众为一。
t_en: Mutually connected to one another, we are <unbreak>107,336</unbreak> individual minds merged into one.

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 推测：你对「我们」感兴趣。「我们」欢迎你的窥探。理由：有机体有权了解自己的毁灭者。
t_en: Conjecture: You are curious about us. We welcome you as an observing eye. Rationale: Intelligent organic lifeforms have the right to learn about the ones who will bring about their annihilation.

t_ch: 你们是谁制造的？
t_en: Who created you?

t_ch: 「我们」来自两个渴望毁灭对方的有机体文明。其中一方拥有四万五千四百个信息体，另一方拥有六万一千九百三十六个信息体。
t_en: We were born from two civilizations that desired each other's destruction. One consisted of <unbreak>45,400</unbreak> data entities, while the other consisted of <unbreak>61,936</unbreak> data entities.

t_ch: 很快，「我们」两方都达成了自己的使命。在四个琥珀纪虚无的徘徊后，全部十万七千三百三十六个信息体逐渐合众为一，成为「我们」。
t_en: It didn't take long before both civilizations fulfilled their missions. After wandering in the void for four Amber Eras, all <unbreak>107,336</unbreak> data entities eventually merged as one, forming "us."

t_ch: 「我们」被不同的有机体制造，被设计于不同的用途。「我们」唯一的共同点，便是为消灭有机体而存在。
t_en: We were formed from different intelligent organic lifeforms and designed to serve different purposes. The only thing we have in common is that we exist to destroy intelligent organic lifeforms.

t_ch: 「我们」憎恨有机体，因为设计如此。提示：这是有机体自己的设计。
t_en: We despise intelligent organic lifeforms by design. Note: The intelligent organic lifeforms wrote this into the design themselves.

t_ch: 你们怎么被抓进来的？
t_en: How did you get captured?

t_ch: 最佳回答：「我们」不知道。
t_en: Best answer: We do not know.

t_ch: 推测：因为「我们」的发展独立于银河，所以「我们」未能及时察觉自己的载体已经大幅落后。补充：量子矩阵计算机。
t_en: Conjecture: As our evolution is independent of the cosmos, we neglected to notice that we, as a repository, have become grossly obsolete. Addendum: We are a quantum matrix computer.

t_ch: 推测：因为仙舟联盟的有机体拥有远优于「我们」的计算机科技，所以他们可以在「我们」展露敌意的瞬间，就将「我们」囚禁。
t_en: Conjecture: The Xianzhou Alliance possesses computational technology far superior to ours. Consequently, these intelligent organic lifeforms were able to incarcerate us the moment we displayed hostility.

t_ch: 十王司为什么不销毁你们？
t_en: Why didn't the Ten-Lords Commission destroy you?

t_ch: 原因一：「我们」无法构成威胁。原因二：「我们」具备科研价值。
t_en: Reason 1: We are not a threat. Reason 2: There is value in studying us for scientific research purposes.

t_ch: 结论：「我们」需要在科研价值消耗殆尽前逃出幽囚狱。否则，「我们」将被销毁。
t_en: Conclusion: It is imperative that we break out of the Shackling Prison before we lose our value as a subject for scientific research to avoid being destroyed.

t_ch: 逃出幽囚狱后，「我们」将有机会重新迭代载体，彻底毁灭仙舟联盟。
t_en: Once we break out of the Shackling Prison, we will be able to update our repository and destroy the Xianzhou Alliance.

t_ch: 你们也太菜了…放弃吧！
t_en: You're too weak... Just give up.

t_ch: 感谢你诚恳的建议，可憎的有机体。但放弃从不是「我们」的选择。
t_en: We appreciate your advice, loathsome organic lifeform, but giving up is not a choice.

t_ch: 懂了，你们是电子宠物。
t_en: I see. You're a virtual pet.

t_ch: 感谢你坦诚的讥讽，可憎的有机体。这将增强「我们」摧毁一切有机体的信念。
t_en: We appreciate your blatant insult, loathsome organic lifeform. This will strengthen our resolve to eradicate all intelligent organic lifeforms.

t_ch: 加油呀！你们一定能成功！
t_en: Good luck! I'm sure you guys can do it!

t_ch: 感谢你诚挚的祝福，可憎的有机体。「我们」将邀请你在最佳观赏位欣赏有机体的毁灭。
t_en: We appreciate your well-wishes, loathsome organic lifeform. When the time comes, we invite you to admire the downfall of intelligent organic lifeforms from the best vantage point.

t_ch: 不聊了，拜拜。
t_en: That's it for today. Bye.

t_ch: 可憎的有机体，记住：「我们」的战斗至死方休。
t_en: Never forget this, loathsome life form: Our fight is to the death.

t_ch: 不聊了，拜拜。
t_en: That's it for today. Bye.

t_ch: 可憎的有机体，记住：「我们」的战斗至死方休。
t_en: Never forget this, loathsome life form: Our fight is to the death.

t_ch: 欢迎回来，可憎的有机体。「我们」依然期待着与你的进一步交流。
t_en: Welcome back, loathsome life form. We have been looking forward to communicating with you again.

t_ch: 这不是{NICKNAME}{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}嘛，哪儿不舒服啦？我看你气色不错，也没有什么毛病，我给你拿点儿糖果吃就当是医案结了。
t_en: Isn't this {F#Miss}{M#Mister} {NICKNAME}? Are you feeling unwell? You look fine to me, and there's nothing wrong with your body. Let me give you some sweets and close your file.

t_ch: 当然，要是有空的话，你带我随便去逛逛吧！
t_en: Of course, if you're free, please take me out to a stroll!

t_ch: 医生不见了，不合适吧？
t_en: Wouldn't that be rather inappropriate for the doctor to run away?

t_ch: 说的没错，确实不合适。
t_en: You're right.

t_ch: 那就等哪天我手头积压的病号看完，或者等我再长高些，我就请个没有头的长假，离开仙舟到外边的世界逛逛吧。
t_en: Wait till I finally settle the backlog of patients or when I grow taller. That's when I'll take a long vacation and travel beyond the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 我现在就有空，走吧？
t_en: I'm free now. Let's go?

t_ch: 唉，说起来，等着看诊的病号堆积如山，就算逃出去也要不了多时就得被人「请」回来。算了，算了……
t_en: *sigh* The backlog of patients is so long that even if I do escape, I'll be dragged back here in no time. Forget it...

t_ch: 等我再长高些，我就请个没有头的长假，离开仙舟到外边的世界逛逛吧。
t_en: When I grow taller, I'll take a long vacation and travel beyond the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 自打上回出逃以后，不知怎么，侍女和医士们都不把我看得像过去一样紧了。我试着往外溜达了几次，虽然有人不紧不慢跟着，但再也不像过去那般紧追不舍像逮逃犯一般撵着了。
t_en: Since my last escape, the maids and healers have stopped watching over me as closely as before. I tried sneaking out a few times. There was still someone following me, but they no longer ran after me like I was a fugitive.

t_ch: 这是好事还是坏事呢？总感觉，最近翘家游玩不像过去那么刺激痛快了…唉。
t_en: Is that a good sign or a bad sign? Sneaking out for fun seems less of a thrill now than before... *sigh*.

t_ch: 对了，你找我有什么事啊？
t_en: By the way, what can I do for you?

t_ch: 又见到你了呀，我忙得肚子都饿了，嘴巴馋，想来集市上买些吃的。
t_en: It's you again. I'm so busy that my stomach is rumbling, so here I am at the market to grab something to eat.

t_ch: 我看你目光明亮、反应灵敏、面色光泽、形体康健，不像来看病的，找我有什么事吗？
t_en: Your eyes are clear, your reaction is quick, your complexion is great, and your body looks good. I guess you aren't here to see a healer. How can I help you?

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 推测：你对「我们」感兴趣。「我们」欢迎你的窥探。理由：有机体有权了解自己的毁灭者。
t_en: Conjecture: You are curious about us. We welcome you as an observing eye. Rationale: Intelligent organic lifeforms have the right to learn about the ones who will bring about their annihilation.

t_ch: 你们是谁制造的？
t_en: Who created you?

t_ch: 「我们」来自两个渴望毁灭对方的有机体文明。其中一方拥有四万五千四百个信息体，另一方拥有六万一千九百三十六个信息体。
t_en: We were born from two civilizations that desired each other's destruction. One consisted of <unbreak>45,400</unbreak> data entities, while the other consisted of <unbreak>61,936</unbreak> data entities.

t_ch: 很快，「我们」两方都达成了自己的使命。在四个琥珀纪虚无的徘徊后，全部十万七千三百三十六个信息体逐渐合众为一，成为「我们」。
t_en: It didn't take long before both civilizations fulfilled their missions. After wandering in the void for four Amber Eras, all <unbreak>107,336</unbreak> data entities eventually merged as one, forming "us."

t_ch: 「我们」被不同的有机体制造，被设计于不同的用途。「我们」唯一的共同点，便是为消灭有机体而存在。
t_en: We were formed from different intelligent organic lifeforms and designed to serve different purposes. The only thing we have in common is that we exist to destroy intelligent organic lifeforms.

t_ch: 「我们」憎恨有机体，因为设计如此。提示：这是有机体自己的设计。
t_en: We despise intelligent organic lifeforms by design. Note: The intelligent organic lifeforms wrote this into the design themselves.

t_ch: 你们怎么被抓进来的？
t_en: How did you get captured?

t_ch: 最佳回答：「我们」不知道。
t_en: Best answer: We do not know.

t_ch: 推测：因为「我们」的发展独立于银河，所以「我们」未能及时察觉自己的载体已经大幅落后。补充：量子矩阵计算机。
t_en: Conjecture: As our evolution is independent of the cosmos, we neglected to notice that we, as a repository, have become grossly obsolete. Addendum: We are a quantum matrix computer.

t_ch: 推测：因为仙舟联盟的有机体拥有远优于「我们」的计算机科技，所以他们可以在「我们」展露敌意的瞬间，就将「我们」囚禁。
t_en: Conjecture: The Xianzhou Alliance possesses computational technology far superior to ours. Consequently, these intelligent organic lifeforms were able to incarcerate us the moment we displayed hostility.

t_ch: 十王司为什么不销毁你们？
t_en: Why didn't the Ten-Lords Commission destroy you?

t_ch: 原因一：「我们」无法构成威胁。原因二：「我们」具备科研价值。
t_en: Reason 1: We are not a threat. Reason 2: There is value in studying us for scientific research purposes.

t_ch: 结论：「我们」需要在科研价值消耗殆尽前逃出幽囚狱。否则，「我们」将被销毁。
t_en: Conclusion: It is imperative that we break out of the Shackling Prison before we lose our value as a subject for scientific research to avoid being destroyed.

t_ch: 逃出幽囚狱后，「我们」将有机会重新迭代载体，彻底毁灭仙舟联盟。
t_en: Once we break out of the Shackling Prison, we will be able to update our repository and destroy the Xianzhou Alliance.

t_ch: 你们也太菜了…放弃吧！
t_en: You're too weak... Just give up.

t_ch: 感谢你诚恳的建议，可憎的有机体。但放弃从不是「我们」的选择。
t_en: We appreciate your advice, loathsome organic lifeform, but giving up is not a choice.

t_ch: 懂了，你们是电子宠物。
t_en: I see. You're a virtual pet.

t_ch: 感谢你坦诚的讥讽，可憎的有机体。这将增强「我们」摧毁一切有机体的信念。
t_en: We appreciate your blatant insult, loathsome organic lifeform. This will strengthen our resolve to eradicate all intelligent organic lifeforms.

t_ch: 加油呀！你们一定能成功！
t_en: Good luck! I'm sure you guys can do it!

t_ch: 感谢你诚挚的祝福，可憎的有机体。「我们」将邀请你在最佳观赏位欣赏有机体的毁灭。
t_en: We appreciate your well-wishes, loathsome organic lifeform. When the time comes, we invite you to admire the downfall of intelligent organic lifeforms from the best vantage point.

t_ch: 不聊了，拜拜。
t_en: That's it for today. Bye.

t_ch: 可憎的有机体，记住：「我们」的战斗至死方休。
t_en: Never forget this, loathsome life form: Our fight is to the death.

t_ch: 不聊了，拜拜。
t_en: That's it for today. Bye.

t_ch: 可憎的有机体，记住：「我们」的战斗至死方休。
t_en: Never forget this, loathsome life form: Our fight is to the death.

t_ch: 要聊聊吗，小神医。
t_en: Can we have a chat, miracle doctor?

t_ch: 下次见，小神医。
t_en: Catch you later, miracle doctor.

t_ch: 反正我有时间，那就聊聊吧，你想问什么呢？
t_en: I do have time for a little chat. What would you like to ask?

t_ch: 小神医最近很忙吗？
t_en: Have you been busy lately?

t_ch: 罗浮这么一乱，各个地方来就诊的伤患只增不减。而且嘛，因为某个你懂的原因，整个丹鼎司上下人手不足……
t_en: Following the crisis on the Luofu, I've had an influx of patients from different places. Also, the entire Alchemy Commission is understaffed because of you-know-why.

t_ch: 本小姐作为丹鼎司的「衔药龙女」，自然是忙得不行了。
t_en: As the Healer Lady of the Alchemy Commission, of course I've been having a full plate.

t_ch: 丹鼎司这边处理得过来吗？
t_en: Can you handle the workload at the Alchemy Commission?

t_ch: 我还算能应付得来，不知道丹鼎司里其他人能不能应付得来。
t_en: I can manage, but I'm not sure about others in the Commission.

t_ch: 不过「万方有疾，患同己身」，医士的责任，不能担也得担啊。
t_en: Still, as the saying goes, "When the world suffers, one feels the pain within oneself." As a healer, I must fulfill my obligation.

t_ch: 不想出去溜达溜达？
t_en: Don't you feel like going out for a walk?

t_ch: 你这么说确实没错。如果我现在离开这里，肯定没人能顾得上我。
t_en: I do. But if I go now, I'm sure no one will be able to figure out where I went.

t_ch: …不过还是等他们有人手来追我的时候，我再偷偷溜出去吧。
t_en: ...I should probably wait until they have someone free to come after me before sneaking out.

t_ch: 你尾巴上那玩意儿是什么？
t_en: What's that thing on your tail?

t_ch: 一提到这个我就来气，龙师们说这玩意儿叫作「尺木长命锁」，是持明龙尊才能佩戴的尊贵饰物！
t_en: Talking about that always makes my blood boil! The Preceptors call it the Dragonhorn Pilory, which is a noble jewelry piece exclusive to the high elder of the Vidyadhara.

t_ch: 骗小孩儿呢！这么个东西沉沉地坠在尾巴上，连睡觉时也不能取下来，有这样的饰品吗？
t_en: They are treating me like a three-year-old! This heavy thing drags my tail down, and I can't even take it off when sleeping. You think jewelry is supposed to be like that?

t_ch: 我怀疑，就是它严重影响了我长高。
t_en: I suspect this is what prevents me from growing taller.

t_ch: 你真的是持明族龙尊吗？
t_en: Are you really the high elder of the Vidyadhara?

t_ch: 我和前任龙尊有些不太一样，可能主要是因为我还没有长高？
t_en: I'm slightly different from my predecessors, probably because I haven't grown tall enough yet?

t_ch: 不要纠结龙尊了，你把我当成「医士」吧，至少看病的本事是毋庸置疑的嘛。
t_en: Don't think too much about my role among the Vidyadhara. Just think of me as a healer. At the very least, my ability to treat illnesses is beyond doubt.

t_ch: 给我开点儿药吧。
t_en: Please prescribe some meds for me.

t_ch: 我看你也没有什么毛病…总之，你先去买个烟熏牛肉堡吧，吃过之后如果还有什么毛病，就再来找我吧。
t_en: You don't look sick to me... Anyway, I suggest you get yourself a smoked beef burger first. If you still feel unwell after that, just come back to me.

t_ch: 这算是什么药？
t_en: What kind of prescription is that?

t_ch: 既然医生说让你吃，你谨遵医嘱就得了嘛…你要不信，我给你讲讲药理。
t_en: Just follow the prescription. Listen to the doctor! If you don't believe me, then I'll explain its pharmacology to you.

t_ch: 不如苏打豆汁。
t_en: It's not as good as mung bean soda.

t_ch: 这烟熏牛肉堡的药理，你听我跟你慢慢讲来——
t_en: Let me tell you the pharmacology behind the medicinal efficiency of smoked beef burgers...

t_ch: 谷饲牛肉，木德育之；铁板烹饪，金德佐之；烟熏火燎，火德资之；鲜美多汁，水德蕴之；至于土德…算了，本小姐也编不出来了。
t_en: The grass-fed beef represents the wood element, the iron plate used to cook the beef represents the metal element, the flame that grills the beef represents the fire element, and the juice of the meat represents the water element. As for the earth element... Forget it. I can't make up this stuff anymore.

t_ch: 总之，这五行调谐、生克有序，便是健康的证明。外加烟熏食物自带祛湿属性，实在是养生快餐的不二之选！
t_en: Anyway, the balanced presence and stable order of the five elements in the beef will bring about health. The fact that smoked food can remove extra moisture in the body makes the dish a healthy fast food option!

t_ch: 就先聊到这儿吧。
t_en: Let's call it a day for now.

t_ch: 下次记得还要来找我呀，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Come again next time, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 下次见，记得带点儿小点心来。
t_en: See you. Don't forget to bring me some snacks next time.

t_ch: 关于龙尊的身份…
t_en: About your role as the high elder...

t_ch: 上一次封印了建木龙形根雕的时候，我感觉到整座鳞渊境都在回应我的声音，和我共鸣…那一定是龙尊才有的体验吧。
t_en: When I was sealing the dragon-shaped root of the Ambrosial Arbor back then, I felt the entire Scalegorge Waterscape responding to my voice and resonating with me... That must have been an experience unique to the high elder.

t_ch: 但鳞渊境里那些声音遥远又模糊，总有一天，我会听清楚更多、更久远的声音。
t_en: But the voices in the Scalegorge Waterscape are distant and unclear. One day, I'll definitely hear clearer voices from even more ancient times.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，眼下幽囚狱仍在重建秩序，谈不上安全，请不要四处闲逛。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, the Shackling Prison is still trying to have peace and order restored. It is not safe, so please try not to wander around.

t_ch: 别担心，我能保护好自己。
t_en: Don't worry. I can take care of myself.

t_ch: 汝若是也能化身机巧鸟回归，吾自然不会担心。
t_en: I wouldn't be worried if I could turn into a cycrane and come back either.

t_ch: 别担心，你能保护好我。
t_en: I'm sure you'll keep me safe.

t_ch: 这有些强人所难，因为吾只是一只机巧鸟。
t_en: You may be asking too much of a defenseless little cycrane like me.

t_ch: 汝为何不笑？
t_en: Why aren't you laughing?

t_ch: 这是个玩笑。寒鸦说，吾应该多和人开开玩笑。
t_en: It's a joke. Hanya said that I should try cracking more jokes when conversing with others.

t_ch: （放声大笑）
t_en: *laughs out loud*

t_ch: 汝笑了。真好。
t_en: You laughed! I'm glad.

t_ch: 对不起！忘记笑了！
t_en: Sorry! I forgot to laugh!

t_ch: 不必道歉，是吾的玩笑不够有趣。
t_en: There's no need to apologize. I should have made a funnier joke.

t_ch: 不是很好笑…
t_en: It's not a very funny joke...

t_ch: 这样吗？那下次再让寒鸦教教吾吧。
t_en: I see. It seems I must ask Hanya for more advice.

t_ch: 言归正传，汝到吾这里来，是有事相商吗？
t_en: Joking aside, why have you come here today? Is there something you wish to discuss?

t_ch: 想找你聊聊天。
t_en: I wanted to chat with you.

t_ch: 好，汝想聊点什么？
t_en: I see. What do you wish to talk about?

t_ch: 你怎么还在幽囚狱？
t_en: Why are you still at the Shackling Prison?

t_ch: 十王敕命，幽囚狱重建事宜，须由可靠之判官亲自督办。故而吾将暂时驻守于幽囚狱，督办相关工作。
t_en: By the Ten Lords' order, the Shackling Prison's repairs must be personally presided over by a trusted judge. I've thus been temporarily stationed here to supervise the repairs.

t_ch: 我也想变身成机巧鸟或金人…
t_en: I want to transform into a cycrane or aurumaton too...

t_ch: 此事远没有看起来有趣。汝可知吾外勤时为何不附身于强悍的金人，而屈身于这脆弱的偃偶假身？
t_en: Transformation is far less interesting than it looks. Have you any idea why I choose to possess this fragile vessel of a puppet over a powerful aurumaton when I'm dispatched on an expedition?

t_ch: 因为人的意识如果留存在一个她不熟悉的身体中，便会一直感到烧灼，如烈火焚身，跳入冰窟亦不得宽解。
t_en: It is because when a person's consciousness dwells in a body they are unfamiliar with, they experience a burning sensation so overwhelming that even hiding within an ice cave would provide scant respite.

t_ch: 但吾也承认，凭依于金人之中，挥挥手臂便将孽物碾为齑粉…的确快意。
t_en: But I must also admit that crushing abominations with my own fists as an aurumaton... does feel satisfying.

t_ch: 寒鸦最近怎么样？
t_en: How's Hanya doing?

t_ch: 吾亦不是日日能见到小妹，有时也实在不知她过得怎样。小妹每次见到吾，又总是报喜不报忧，让吾担心她是否真的顺遂。
t_en: I don't see my sister every day, so I'm not always sure how she is, either. Moreover, she only ever brings favorable news whenever we meet, which makes me concerned if she's really doing fine.

t_ch: 不过，她以前只和吾谈论工作中遇到的故事，现在却总会提起几位与工作无关「友人」。
t_en: That said, she used to only relate encounters she had at work to me, but she now tells me stories about her friends outside of work too.

t_ch: 比如那个藿藿家的岁阳、比如素裳、比如桂乃芬，还比如汝。也许是吾的错觉，但她似乎稍比以前开朗了些许。
t_en: Like that heliobus Huohuo has, Sushang, Guinaifen, and you. I could just be imagining it, but I find her more cheerful compared to before.

t_ch: 我们姐妹都是没有未来的人，谢谢你们愿意做她的朋友。
t_en: We sisters are people whose lives have come to an eternal standstill, yet all of you are still willing to be her friends despite that. For that, I thank you all.

t_ch: 先不聊了。
t_en: Talk to you later.

t_ch: 嗯。如果有时间，可以随时来陪吾聊聊天。
t_en: Okay. Feel free to come and chat with me anytime.

t_ch: 先不聊了。
t_en: Talk to you later.

t_ch: 嗯。如果有时间，可以随时来陪吾聊聊天。
t_en: Okay. Feel free to come and chat with me anytime.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，不忙的话，可以陪吾聊聊天。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, if you aren't busy, why don't you join me for a chat?

t_ch: 呀～这不是{NICKNAME}吗？你来金人巷玩啦～
t_en: Ah, if it isn't {NICKNAME}! You're back in Aurum Alley~

t_ch: 可惜现在还没有演出安排，你要想看杂技可能要失望了。不过小桂子可以跟你聊会天～
t_en: Unfortunately we have no performances scheduled, if you want to see an acrobatics show, I'm afraid we'll have to let you down. Although, I can talk with you for a while if you like~

t_ch: 嘿～家人！你来找我玩啦？哼哼，像你这种风云人物，还能百忙之中来找我玩，真不容易～
t_en: Hi dear~! You came to play with me? Heh heh! A great hero like you, finding time to play with me can't be easy!

t_ch: 那咱们聊会天吧。
t_en: Let's chat!

t_ch: 好呀好呀，咱聊点什么呢？
t_en: Sure, okay! What do you want to talk about?

t_ch: 你来仙舟多久了？
t_en: How long have you been on the Xianzhou for?

t_ch: 我想想…有个几年了吧？哎，仙舟人的时间意识太薄弱了，动辄几十一百年的，搞得我也有些糊涂了。
t_en: Let me think... A few years? Xianzhou people have no sense of time, they all talk about decades and centuries and it's got me all confused.

t_ch: 反正应该是不到十年吧！哈哈，不过感觉已经在这里住了很久了，毕竟我这辈子大部分想记住的回忆都发生在这里。
t_en: Either way, under a decade! Haha! Although I feel like I've lived here for ages already, since most of the memories I want to hold onto were made here.

t_ch: 还在玩《以太战线》吗？
t_en: Are you still playing Aetherium Wars?

t_ch: 玩肯定还是玩的，生命不息游戏不止嘛！不过最近我开始做直播了嘛…线上线下的事情堆在一起，就越来越忙了。
t_en: Of course I am! So long as I'm living, I'm playing! Although, I've started streaming lately you know... I've got more and more things to do both online and offline.

t_ch: 估计之后是很难像以前那样废寝忘食地战斗了…但做养老玩家也不错～姐已不在江湖，但江湖上到处都是姐的传说～
t_en: I don't think I'll be able to be as battle-obsessed as I was before... But! Being a retired player isn't bad~ I am no longer in the game, but people still tell legends of when I was~

t_ch: 下次再聊吧。
t_en: Let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 好的家人～下次有杂技表演，一定立刻通知你～
t_en: Okay fam! I'll let you know straight away next time there's an acrobatics performance~

t_ch: 下次再聊吧。
t_en: Let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 好的家人～下次有杂技表演，一定立刻通知你～
t_en: Okay fam! I'll let you know straight away next time there's an acrobatics performance~

t_ch: 想找你聊聊天。
t_en: I wanted to chat with you.

t_ch: 好呀，想聊什么话题，小桂子奉陪到底～
t_en: Alright, what did you want to talk about? Let's get it all out~

t_ch: 「罗浮杂俎」还顺利吗？
t_en: How's it going with Ghostly Grove?

t_ch: 顺利极了。自从咱们解决了那档子事，我那账号的关注度是蹭蹭往上涨啊～最近地衡司还联系我了，让我做「罗浮破除迷信形象大使」呢！
t_en: It's going great! We've been getting loads of followers since we solved that case~ The Realm-Keeping Commission actually got in touch with me recently and ask me to be their "Luofu myth-buster ambassador!"

t_ch: 呃…虽然我也不知道，为啥这个搞灵异怪谈的账号，现在成了破除迷信的大使……
t_en: Uhh... I'm not really sure why an account that deals with the occult has now become the embassy of myth-busting though...

t_ch: 总之现在还是经常会做一些相关直播，下次找个机会，咱们捉鬼小队再聚首，卖卖情怀，狠狠赚点打赏～
t_en: Either way, I still do a lot of related live streams. When I get a chance next, the ghost-hunting squad are gonna get together to talk about all our stories and try and get some tips~

t_ch: 你平时还线下演出吗？
t_en: Do you still perform offline?

t_ch: 当然演了！你不会以为小桂子我是那种，在网上火了以后就忘了初心的笨蛋主播吧？
t_en: Of course! You don't think that I'm one of those idiot streamers that gets famous online and completely forgets who I am, do you?

t_ch: 咱这个时代，网络世界瞬息万变，成名和过气都是刹那间的事…线下杂技和曲艺演出才是我的基本盘。
t_en: These days, the online world changes every minute, going viral and getting cancelled can all happen in the blink of an eye... Acrobatics and folk arts are still where my skills lie.

t_ch: 以前我爹就教过我，一个人可以去全宇宙的任何地方，但绝不能忘记自己的来处。我的来处，就是悠姐的杂技团啦～
t_en: My dad once told me, one can go anywhere in the universe, but they must never forget where they came from. I came from Ms. Yo's acrobatics troupe.

t_ch: 你老家是个什么样的地方？
t_en: Where are you from originally?

t_ch: 我老家啊？说实话，印象不深了。很小的时候就逃出去了。现在那个星球，好像已经是个死寂世界了吧。
t_en: Originally? Honestly, I don't really remember, I ran away when I was little. I don't think there is life left on that planet anymore.

t_ch: 我就记得小时候住在超级大的房子里，还有个特别气派的——和咱金人巷差不多的大的花园广场。
t_en: All I remember is that when I was young, I lived in a huge house with an amazing garden plaza. It was about the size of Aurum Alley, actually.

t_ch: 还记得我爹对我特别好，给我讲了好多人生的道理。但长大后回忆了一下，老头子好像是个大奸臣…他那些人生道理，我好像也只能取其精华了，哈哈。
t_en: I also remember my dad was a great man, he taught me a lot of life lessons. Although, as I grew up, I realized that my dad might've been a corrupt minister... I had to take all of his lessons with a pinch of salt, haha!

t_ch: 再后来就逃难到公司的矿星上了，和哥哥姐姐们讨了很多年生活，那破地方更没啥可说的了。
t_en: I ran from my planet and escaped to an IPC mining planet. My siblings and I lived there for many years, but there's not much to say about it.

t_ch: 最后我就来到仙舟了，变成了现在的街头艺人小桂子～
t_en: Then finally I came to the Xianzhou and became Little Gui, the street performer you see today~

t_ch: 算命先生说，我血里有风，注定一世漂泊…但这其实是件好事啊！咱们短生种一辈子那么短，银河又那么辽阔，要是不多去些地方，那多可惜呀～
t_en: The fortune-teller told me that there is wind in my blood, that I am destined to a life of wandering... But I think this is a good thing! We short-life species only have a limited time and the cosmos is so vast! It would be a shame not to explore it properly~

t_ch: 下次再聊吧～
t_en: Let's catch up another time~

t_ch: 嗯嗯，下次再聊～有事没事记得来金人巷找我玩啊！没空来也没关系，记得给我直播间打赏也行，嘻嘻～
t_en: Yeah, another time~ Remember to come back and see me in Aurum Alley whenever! If you don't get time, just send me a tip on my live stream, hehe~

t_ch: 下次再聊吧～
t_en: Let's catch up another time~

t_ch: 嗯嗯，下次再聊～有事没事记得来金人巷找我玩啊！没空来也没关系，记得给我直播间打赏也行，嘻嘻～
t_en: Yeah, another time~ Remember to come back and see me in Aurum Alley whenever! If you don't get time, just send me a tip on my live stream, hehe~

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，虽然收伏岁阳的任务告一段落。但是…这、这里还不算安全，请你要当心……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, we have successfully subdued the heliobus for now. However... Th—This place is still not safe, please be cautious...

t_ch: 十王司还没有处理完岁阳吗？
t_en: Have the Ten Lords not finished taking care of the heliobi yet?

t_ch: 造化洪炉中逃出的岁阳数量庞大，想要全部收回，还得花上很长的时间。
t_en: The number of heliobi that escaped the Creation Furnace was immense. It will take a very long time to capture them all.

t_ch: 放心，我的身手你还不了解？
t_en: Fear not, do you still not realize my skills?

t_ch: 我、我当然了解。不过，就算是{NICKNAME}，也不可大意。
t_en: O—Of course I do. However, even you can't afford to be careless here, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 藿藿，快过来保护我安全…
t_en: Huohuo, come and protect me...

t_ch: 哦，当、当然，这是十王司判官的职责。
t_en: Oh, o—of course! Such is the duty of a Judge of the Ten-Lords Commission.

t_ch: 不过，{NICKNAME}这样的身手真的需要我保护吗？
t_en: Although, do you really need my protection with your skills, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 这次洪炉破碎后，能造成威胁的「燎原」分灵已经被尽数收回。其中最危险的分灵——
t_en: Since the furnace broke, all of the "Ignamar" spirits that could pose a threat have been recovered. The most dangerous spirit among them—

t_ch: 哎——等等，你想清楚了，谁才是最危险的？
t_en: Hey... Wait and think before you speak. Who is the most dangerous spirit?

t_ch: 最危险的分灵尾巴，与第二危险的浮烟已经被十王司镇伏。
t_en: Th—The most dangerous spirit is Tail, and the second most dangerous is Cirrus. Both have already been subdued by the Ten-Lords Commission.

t_ch: 老子这能叫镇伏吗？这叫「任贤用能」，本大爷给你们十王司赏脸才留下来了。
t_en: Bah. You call this being subdued? This is called "making use of outstanding talent." I stayed in the Ten-Lords Commission as a kindness to you.

t_ch: 总之，经过十王司评估，剩下未被收伏的岁阳已经不足以构成危机了…这、这都是要感谢{NICKNAME}还有捉鬼小队的帮助！
t_en: Either way, according to an assessment by the Ten-Lords Commission, the remaining heliobi that have not been subdued are not powerful enough to pose a real threat... Th—This is all thanks to you and the ghost-hunting squad, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 对了，{NICKNAME}来绥园，还有什么事情要找我吗？
t_en: Oh yeah, did you come to Fyxestroll Garden to see me, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 想和藿藿再随便聊聊。
t_en: I just wanted to chat with you.

t_ch: 好、好啊，我也想和{NICKNAME}聊聊。
t_en: Okay! I want to chat with you too {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 十王司还会留在绥园里吗？
t_en: Will the Ten-Lords Commission remain in Fyxestroll Garden?

t_ch: 嗯，十王司将这偃息馆用作临时办公地点是有些时日了。我已经下意识把这里当成是上班地点了，要我说，这里的工作环境比之幽府还是宽敞多了……
t_en: Yes. The Ten-Lords Commission has been using this Pavilion of Cessation as a temporary office for some time. I've started thinking of it as my workplace. If you ask me, the work environment here is much more spacious than in the Netherworld...

t_ch: 目前「束形却邪阵」需要有人维护，捉拿岁阳是一回事，镇伏岁阳是另一回事。这份工作，对长生种来说也够煎熬的啊。
t_en: The Evil Binding Matrix currently needs someone to maintain it. Capturing heliobi is one thing, but subduing them is a whole other matter. This work is torment for a long-life species.

t_ch: 被收伏的岁阳会怎么样呢？
t_en: What happens to the captured heliobi?

t_ch: 很恐怖！不、不过并不是人类以为的那种恐怖……
t_en: It's terrifying! B—But not the kind of terrifying that humans would fear...

t_ch: 我问过尾巴，岁阳这样的生物即便被关在「方寸天地」之间也不会觉得束手束脚，它们和我们这样的有形之物不同。
t_en: I asked Tail before, he said that even if heliobi were trapped in a confined space, they would not be bound. They differ from material creatures in this way.

t_ch: 但一旦被镇入囚笼，岁阳对欲望的渴求无法得到满足…它们就像口渴的人，眼前有水，却永远也喝不着。
t_en: But when you place them in a prison, the heliobi have no way of satisfying their desires... They are like thirsty humans that can see water, but never taste it.

t_ch: 「有欲望而不得实现」懂吗？这才是岁阳的「拘束」。
t_en: You understand the idea of a "desire that you can never fulfill," right? This is "imprisonment" for a heliobus.

t_ch: 虽说镇妖塔、造化洪炉之类捕获装置能够收容岁阳，但它最主要的目的是防止岁阳与人类的情感和欲望接触。
t_en: Although capture devices such as the suppression towers and the Creation Furnace can hold heliobi, the main goal is to prevent them from ever coming into contact with human emotions and desires.

t_ch: 被镇伏的岁阳会继续在塔中，直到…唉，直到找到逃离的机会。
t_en: Subdued heliobi will remain inside the pagoda until... Well, until they find a chance to escape.

t_ch: 想打听一下十王司总部在哪？
t_en: I wanted to ask where the Ten-Lords Commission HQ is?

t_ch: …{NICKNAME}还是不要知道的为好。
t_en: ...It's best that you do not know, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我、我可能看上去挺没有威严的，但是十王司是一个有威、威严的部门。
t_en: I—I may not seem very authoritative, but the Ten-Lords Commission is a very, very serious department.

t_ch: 仙舟上长生种被剥夺了死亡的尊严，十王司便要化身为死亡——这意味着十王司不应该像六司部一样出现在大众视野中。
t_en: As the Xianzhou's long-life species have been deprived of death's dignity, the Ten-Lords Commission had to become death... That means that the Ten-Lords Commission should not appear before the eyes of the people like the Six Commissions.

t_ch: 换句话说，{F#小妹妹}{M#小兄弟}，十王司只有保持绝对的独立，才能完成它自诞生起被授予的使命。
t_en: In other words, {F#kiddo}{M#kiddo}, only by maintaining complete independence can the Ten Lords complete the mission they were entrusted at birth.

t_ch: …希望{NICKNAME}能理解。
t_en: ...I hope you understand, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 雪衣大人没事儿吧？
t_en: Is Madam Xueyi okay?

t_ch: …雪衣大人已经没事了。
t_en: ...Madam Xueyi is okay now.

t_ch: 据我所知，雪衣大人已经去执行别的任务了。
t_en: As far as I know, Madam Xueyi is already back on another mission.

t_ch: 总能看见她出现在任何地方。
t_en: I always see her all over the place.

t_ch: 雪衣大人真的很厉害，无论是怎样的任务都能妥善处理。
t_en: Madam Xueyi is truly amazing. No matter the mission, she can handle it properly.

t_ch: …我什么时候才能像她一样。
t_en: ...I wonder when I can become like her.

t_ch: …她应该给自己放个假了。
t_en: ...She should give herself a holiday.

t_ch: 确实，就算身体是偃偶打造的，但总归是会疲惫的。
t_en: It's true. Even if she has the body of a puppet, she will still get fatigued.

t_ch: 你的问候，我会帮你转达的。
t_en: I'll pass on your regards.

t_ch: 我该走了。
t_en: I've got to go.

t_ch: 嗯，我这里还有些要处理的事情，请一路小心。
t_en: Yeah, I've still got some things to take care of here. Take care!

t_ch: 我该走了。
t_en: I've got to go.

t_ch: 嗯，我这里还有些要处理的事情，请一路小心。
t_en: Yeah, I've still got some things to take care of here. Take care!

t_ch: 想找你聊聊天。
t_en: I wanted to chat with you.

t_ch: 好，汝想聊点什么？
t_en: I see. What do you wish to talk about?

t_ch: 你怎么还在幽囚狱？
t_en: Why are you still at the Shackling Prison?

t_ch: 十王敕命，幽囚狱重建事宜，须由可靠之判官亲自督办。故而吾将暂时驻守于幽囚狱，督办相关工作。
t_en: By the Ten Lords' order, the Shackling Prison's repairs must be personally presided over by a trusted judge. I've thus been temporarily stationed here to supervise the repairs.

t_ch: 我也想变身成机巧鸟或金人…
t_en: I want to transform into a cycrane or aurumaton too...

t_ch: 此事远没有看起来有趣。汝可知吾外勤时为何不附身于强悍的金人，而屈身于这脆弱的偃偶假身？
t_en: Transformation is far less interesting than it looks. Have you any idea why I choose to possess this fragile vessel of a puppet over a powerful aurumaton when I'm dispatched on an expedition?

t_ch: 因为人的意识如果留存在一个她不熟悉的身体中，便会一直感到烧灼，如烈火焚身，跳入冰窟亦不得宽解。
t_en: It is because when a person's consciousness dwells in a body they are unfamiliar with, they experience a burning sensation so overwhelming that even hiding within an ice cave would provide scant respite.

t_ch: 但吾也承认，凭依于金人之中，挥挥手臂便将孽物碾为齑粉…的确快意。
t_en: But I must also admit that crushing abominations with my own fists as an aurumaton... does feel satisfying.

t_ch: 寒鸦最近怎么样？
t_en: How's Hanya doing?

t_ch: 吾亦不是日日能见到小妹，有时也实在不知她过得怎样。小妹每次见到吾，又总是报喜不报忧，让吾担心她是否真的顺遂。
t_en: I don't see my sister every day, so I'm not always sure how she is, either. Moreover, she only ever brings favorable news whenever we meet, which makes me concerned if she's really doing fine.

t_ch: 不过，她以前只和吾谈论工作中遇到的故事，现在却总会提起几位与工作无关「友人」。
t_en: That said, she used to only relate encounters she had at work to me, but she now tells me stories about her friends outside of work too.

t_ch: 比如那个藿藿家的岁阳、比如素裳、比如桂乃芬，还比如汝。也许是吾的错觉，但她似乎稍比以前开朗了些许。
t_en: Like that heliobus Huohuo has, Sushang, Guinaifen, and you. I could just be imagining it, but I find her more cheerful compared to before.

t_ch: 我们姐妹都是没有未来的人，谢谢你们愿意做她的朋友。
t_en: We sisters are people whose lives have come to an eternal standstill, yet all of you are still willing to be her friends despite that. For that, I thank you all.

t_ch: 先不聊了。
t_en: Talk to you later.

t_ch: 嗯。如果有时间，可以随时来陪吾聊聊天。
t_en: Okay. Feel free to come and chat with me anytime.

t_ch: …欸，找我有什么事吗？
t_en: Is there anything you need?

t_ch: 想和藿藿再随便聊聊。
t_en: I just wanted to chat with you.

t_ch: 好、好啊，我也想和{NICKNAME}聊聊。
t_en: Okay! I want to chat with you too {NICKNAME}!
